reviews.review_description:
So i bought and started playing this game thinking it would be a short little one shot arcade adventure... I HAD NO IDEA HOW WRONG I WAS AND HOW PEAK THIS GAME IS🤯🤯🤯
for starters, open world in vr is awsome, the graphics are gorgeous, the story is gripping, the missions, while repetitive are fun and risky af, the combat is fun and frenzied if caught off guard, the weapons handle like a dream and the ending... AH THE ENDING... i cried. *SPOILER WARNING* (I picked the self sacrifice ending. That last frame before the credits had me bawling😭😭😭) 11/10 the best vr experience in my life thus far, with a very close 2nd place to blade and sorcery with starwars mods. To the devs, i say: "thank you. Thank you for the great story and gameplay. Thank you for pouring so much love and passion into your game. Thank you for feeding our imaginations."

Into the radius 1 is an amazing game it has spooky moments and balanced enemys the only thing mid thing is, is how lonely it is i mean yeah its a  survival game but it sucks that you dont have like a dog atleast a dog like dogmeat from fallout or like an anomaly dog  but like how u said its way better then the second, I feel like the second the mimics do to much damage and are OP especially for beginners I had to turn down every setting on them and they still killed me in less then 5 taps so I haven't played in a while but anyways good game.

When I first played this game I thought it was gonna be like creepy horror with jump scares and limited gunplay and level based gameplay but I was glad this game proved my thoughts wrong because man this game is one of if not the best VR games I've ever played as a guy who likes gunplay I've got to give it to into the radius it has the best gunplay I've seen in a VR game, it's everything I could've hoped for, man it's too bad I can't give this game a higher rating than 5 best VR game out there. Best gunplay and open world game out of any other.

I’ve been on vr for a few years now and I’ve never had as good of an experience as this. Wonderful gun mechanics and diversity, great save system and exploration. It doesn’t matter how heavy hearted you are, this game will scare you, I have screamed in fear at least 7 times. The story is okay enough to be into it, the replayability for me is peak. Other than that it took me 40 total hours of game time to finish the story. I’ve put over 100 hours into it and am still entertained. This game is so incredibly immersive you will find yourself playing until your headset dies. Well worth it for an UNDER priced game.

reviews.review_title: This is THE VR Survival game
reviews.review_description:
Not frustrating and you never feel cheated. (Kinda) 
Such a brilliant experience Survival 10/10 Gunplay 10/10 Enemies 10/10 
Kinda confusing at first for new players however if you PAY ATTENTION it'll be amazing
The atmosphere is matched by no other vr game and all the negatives reviews are lying

reviews.review_title: This game is a failure in optimization.
reviews.review_description:
This game is poorly optimized for Quest 3 standalone. Frame drops occur constantly. In complex areas—such as the Pobeda Factory—the frame rate plummets from 50 FPS to as low as 30 FPS. In certain specific zones, likely due to bugs, the frame rate drops to 10 FPS, causing extreme lag that makes normal gameplay impossible.

​I own three separate Quest 3 units, all of which are brand new and in perfect condition. Since I am experiencing the exact same issues on all three devices, this is clearly not a hardware defect on my end.

​Furthermore, I attempted to prioritize performance using Quest Games Optimizer (QGO) by:
​Reducing the resolution by 10% (below the base 1680).
​Setting CPU and GPU levels to "Full."​
Increasing FFR (Fixed Foveated Rendering) to Level 2.

​Despite these adjustments, there was no noticeable improvement. This is a clear failure in game optimization. Dealing with the stuttering and lag is exhausting and incredibly frustrating; it completely breaks the immersion.

​The developers need to admit that the optimization has failed. I am unsure if the performance has degraded because the Quest 3 OS has become "heavier" due to recent continuous updates, but regardless, the current state is unacceptable. Considering the actual performance limits of the Quest 3, the developers should at least provide users with an option to lower the graphic settings. I would much rather play with simpler graphics if it meant a smooth, fluid experience.

I think bots are driving up the rating, but it doesn't matter. The game has nothing to do with anything, probably one of the worst games I've ever played. The only thing that's good is the graphics. One star is just for the graphics, otherwise zero.

I love majority of this game, the guns are great but, why does the stamina and sprinting have to be so awful, I get it, it’s a survival game but keep all the other survival aspects but just not stamina, the walking is so slow it takes forever to go anywhere, maybe fast travel or unlocking something to have infinite or almost infinite sprint, even adding a vehicle for those who don’t care how much noise they’re making, I’d love to keep playing but the sprinting is so annoying I don’t even want to progress further, especially when I need to travel so far just to get some artefact ￼

developer_response.body: Hi there explorer! Thanks for your review and time put into the game. ITR1 was developed a few years ago and is finished, so we're not working on it any more. We do not know how much you've played, but in general as you progress, you'll unlock 2 ways shortcuts from the base to Bolotki, Kolkhoz and Pervomay route. Pobeda will be the only main location in the end that you won't be able to reach directly from the base and instead through another location.

reviews.review_description: From getting this game in 2022, completing it, revisiting it and beating it again (pause) in 2024, to re-revisiting it and beating it again earlier this year shows how peak this game is for me to play 4 years later

reviews.review_title: I was totally wrong ,so so SO WRONG
reviews.review_description:
This is  THE VR experience for anyone looking to try vr for the first time, or anyone wanting to ACTUALLY immerse themselves into a world, this is it, it has one thing that stalker lacked for me , HOARDING and having a place TO STORE/DISPLAY all your loot like whats the point in looting if i cant get the sense that not only am i actually accomplishing something, but being compensated as well i got a quest 3s a month ago, never having tried vr, and as someone whos never tried it other than the cheap lil boxes you put your phone in (apparently the quest 3s is basically just a phone you wear on your face tech wise i guess)
THIS GAME should be one of the main ones you let someone try to show them what VR CAN BE , dude the weapon manipulation is awesome, it feels sooo good , like dude i find myself cleaning and organizing my vr house more than my own lol , like it makes you want to stay in that world, hey devs, maybe you could conjure up some more magic and find other ways for the player to spend more time in your world or invest more time into the world.....it would be cool if they had tvs kinda like in max payne and gta, you could use them as just a plot device like they do in max payne or just have some silly stuff to watch like gta, or could use both, it could be like old hitatchi tube tv's as only certain tech can function in the radius , youve already got the whole , paranormal/para-dimensional feel going on, you could lean into that as well as the realism, like every that has to do with the player and all their stuff is treated with such a love for detail and realism and then have the world around them just be abnormal as heck , i havent played the second one yet, but i feel like its the real radius, as in the second one was the version you guys wanted to make maybe, but that being said, thats not a slight at the first one, i really love this game, i can tell the game i want to play is the second one though, basically the game makes me not want to take the headset off, and especially since im still using the head strap the quest comes with THATS A HIGH COMPLEMENT,THANK YOU GUYS FOR ALL YOUR HARD WORK, AND OBVIOUS LOVE FOR GAMING
I THINK THATS THE PROBLEM TODAY, THE INDUSTRY IS FULL OF THE SAME PPL THAT BACK IN SCHOOL WOULD HAVE MADE FUN OF YOU FOR GAMING OR READING COMICS, NOW THOSE SAME TYPES ARE THE INDUSTRY, ITS PART OF WHY THEY HATE GAMERS , THAT AND THEY RESENT THE FACT THEY LANDED IN GAMING OR GAMING JOURNALISM, THEY DONT WANT TO BE THERE THEY THOUGHT THEY WERE GONNA END UP AT THE NEW YORK TIMES, AND THEY LOOK AT THE CONSUMER LIKE ITS THEIR FAULT, THE PPL MAKING GAMES TODAY HATE GAMING , REMEMBER ABOUT TEN YEARS AGO WHEN THEY HAD ALL THOSE "BECOME A GAME DESIGNER" ADS
WELL THATS HOW WE GOT OUR CURRENT CLIMATE, 
So from the bottom of my heart, thank you
Thank you guys for GIVING A DARN
yours truly
Original gamer
This game has amazing mechanics good visuals and phenomenal gameplay i love the survival aspect and the wanderer tone it has.

And don’t let 1 star players get down  devs your great im glad you’re making a 2 i this on pc and quest so think you for so many good  memories of this game and some how this game has got me trough some of my worst  times  so thanks for this game devs
￼

The atmosphere is immersive and uniquely dark. Walking around in a post apocalyptic waste land where creepy dark figures chase you around is a really cool concept. My biggest gripe with the game is the inventory system. I like how immersive it is to physically pull your backpack out and meticulously place every item in it but the immersion runs out when it becomes tedious. When you go an a long loot run you will have a lot of big and small items that you have to place on top of other items all while making sure you can still access your magazines, ammunition boxes, and single bullets in case something is sneaking up on you. I don’t dislike the challenge it adds but it takes up a lot of time, eventually you will spend more time managing your loot then looking around. And if you die good luck picking everything up one by one. Everything else makes up for this blunder so it’s not a deal breaker for me but adding an option for a simple grid style inventory would’ve been a good call.
Personly I didn't like it some how as a grown woman I got kind of scared at the first part and it did make me a little bit Motion sick, but the guns and reloading are pretty good

I got the supporter edition and my camos aren't showing up in-game. There was no camo pm in my hub and I cant apply them to any guns they should be able to be applied to in the repair booth. Helpp!!!

Edit for dev reply: You ignored what I even said. I know how to apply them; the dlc just never activated. I own this game on pcvr also with the supporter bundle.... I just refunded this because of how rude the community was to me on the discord. Good luck with the second game..

developer_response.body: Hi there, explorer. If you've completed the tutorial, you should see camo version of PM in the player hub. You also should be able to apply camo via Upgrade box on the base to compatible 5 guns (depending which are available according to your security level) PM (Pistol), AKS-74U (Carbine), IZh-81 (Pump-action Shotgun), OTs-33 (Pistol), Mosin-Nagant (Sniper Rifle).

developer_response.body: Thank you for trying the game, Explorer! Most of the actions in the game are manual, so the best thing to do is just keep playing and get used to it. All the interactions, such as reloading, taking care of items, ammo and food, and organising your inventory, are manual. Hopefully, it will all start to feel more familiar and work better for you in future playthroughs if you decide to give the game another chance.

reviews.review_description: don't know what to put here but it's it is amazing :)

PLEASE a Arachnophobia mode that gets rid of those spawns. The game's a great game. the most immersive, in depth survival game, with the best mechanics I've ever seen in all of VR. I'd be doing a disservice if I didn't give this five stars, I'm not going to lower the games review because of my personal skill issues. The game is really fun and immersive, small tip though, save your game regularly! Before adventuring out, after a fight, maybe before you go artifact hunting? Just to save yourself some time incase you die. <3

the stamina in a game drains so ridiculously fast it's a vr game i'm already working and moving my body parts in real life to play the game why is there a stamina bar there's no need to is this makes my character get tired every 5 seconds he's barely moving. give the character more stamina or just remove it entirely i get that you want the game to be realistic but for a vr game there need you can put a hunger bar but not a stamina one because in real life i don't care how tired you are if you're getting shot at i guarantee you you'll be moving fast.

other than that i love the game i wish there was more meds and health packs because you take so much damage from getting hit 

and a brightness meter because the game is ridiculously dark i get that it's a horror game but i would like to have more options in the settings if i want to make the game brighter or darker.

THE GAME DOESN'T EVEN LOAD ANYMORE!!!! I'VE TRIED EVERYTHING AND IT WONT WORK!!! THE DEVS ARE JUST KEEPING THIS AS A CASH GRAB BECAUSE THEY ARENT EVEN FIXING IT!!! THEY ARENT EVEN ACKNOWLEDGING THE COMMUNITY THAT ALLOWED THEM TO BE SUCCESSFUL!!!!!!!

developer_response.body: Hello, explorer. We haven't make any changes to the game for months, so if it doesn't load, it's not caused by update of the game.  In any case, this is what you can try – please, visit headset settings – general – storage – find ITR in the list and clear app's data (it's a button on the left side from uninstall option.)? This should remove save files (!), but also reset the game in general to defaults. If this doesn't help, reach out to us directly support@cm-immersive.com	so we could take a better look.

Im a big fan of the stalker/metro saga and having been able to play a game like this, that not only honors those classics but also manages to stand out on its own, has been great.
This game has been made with love, passion, attention to detail... all the good things you can name. If you don't want to cry on your knees from how intense and hard stuff in this game can get (like me) you can always tweak everything in the gameplay and difficulty to your liking.
This game has managed to be up there with all time great VR titles, cause it is. Play it.
Got to love a good ol’ Roadside Picnic.

I don’t scare easily. I’m a 39-year-old grown man who knows very well this is just a game… and yet Into the Radius consistently makes me feel like an 8-year-old kid walking alone through a haunted forest, convinced every shadow is about to grab me.
And that’s exactly why I keep coming back.
Time and time again I swear I’m done. I rip off the headset, heart pounding, neck hairs standing straight up, muttering: “Never again.”
Then a few hours later? I’m back in the Radius.
The mechanics are absolutely phenomenal — but be warned: they take some getting used to. More than once, in the most intense, life-or-death moments, I’ve accidentally hit the mag drop button or pulled out the completely wrong item from my backpack while panic set in. It’s frustrating, hilarious, and oddly realistic all at the same time. And honestly? It just adds to the chaos and immersion.
Gun handling feels weighty, realistic and deeply satisfying. Reloading under pressure, maintaining your gear, planning your route — it all adds to an intensely immersive experience that VR rarely achieves at this level.
Visually, the game is more than solid, and with the optimizer enabled it becomes genuinely beautiful. Even small details, like the beam of your flashlight slicing through the darkness, are stunning. That cone of light alone delivers more atmosphere than most horror games manage in their entirety.
Do I fully understand the story yet? Not really. But the gameplay is so gripping that it hardly matters. For the past two weeks, this has been the only game I’ve played daily — despite the fact it scares the hell out of me every single session.
Into the Radius isn’t just a VR game.
It’s an experience. A nerve-shredding, heart-racing, deeply addictive experience.
If you love immersion, tension and realistic survival mechanics — welcome to your new obsession. Just don’t blame me when you start sleeping with the lights on.
If I could read this game higher, I would this is the best game I’ve ever played in my whole entire life this game deserves like a movie on it, bro ￼

I've had this game for a little over 2 years now, each playthrough comes with something different. Its a gorgeous game. Good graphics, little-no bugs, actually scary unlike other horror or fear-factor games

Maybe this game is good but the first time I played it literally took me 15 minutes to turn the safety off on my gun and I have no idea how I actually did it. I just kept  pressing every combination of buttons imaginable. ￼ Last time I checked games are supposed to be fun not frustrating and annoying ￼, plus the graphics suck.

Great game! The lore and action packed moments are crazy! This is by far one of the most immersive vr experience I have played and my only criticism is  the “A Farewell Feast” mission bugged out on me making it so I could no longer progress. I placed all of the items on the red cloth table and I forgot a detector. I had to leave so I then saved my progress by exiting to main menu and when I returned with a detector, there was no artifact on the red cloth table. Sadly that run was iroman so I could not load an auto save and now I have to restart a run I have spent 30+ hours on.

This game is a solo, mission oriented game. I don't play a lot of video games, but the ones I enjoy most are Population One and Sims 4. I also have a niche interest in dytopian, post-apocalyptic movies and shows. This game kinda melds all those interests for me. 

I frequently encounter new enemies with unique skills or features I have to combat. As I play, I am slowly building a personal arsenal and skill set which will helps me as I move along in the game. It draws me into the world so immersively many hours will pass without me realizing it, and I've often kicked myself for not being a productive human being. But hey, at least my character has been productive. That makes it better, right?

If you like a generally peaceful (and eerie) slow-paced game with occasional battles, and you don't mind an occasional jump scare and playing alone, then you'll thoroughly enjoy this game.
This is great. I'll finish this then buy the 2nd.

developer_response.body:
Hello, explorer. There's distant grab for fetching items that you are unable to reach. The item in question (you look at) is highlighted. 

If there's bending over, it can happen when an item over a table or some other surface that is raised but not on the floor level, so when you bend, it might push you back physics-wise.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the review, Explorer. If you experience issues or have questions, you can always post on our Discord (discord.gg/itr) or Reddit (r/intotheradius), and other Explorers will try to help you out. If the game doesn't run well or something else technical happens, reach out to us directly at support@cm-immersive.com.

developer_response.body: Hey! Start a new game and do not skip tutorial and eventually you should figure it all out! Also do not hesitate to ask for help on our socials: discord.gg/itr or Reddit - /r/intotheradius.

reviews.review_title: Great game that could  been improved
reviews.review_description:
The game is enjoyable, offering tense and scary moments. However, after a few hours, it feels repetitive. The absence of certain NPCs can make the game feel a bit gloomy.

My main criticisms are the difficulty in storing items in the pouch and locating things in the backpack. Additionally, it’s easy to lose your gear, including guns. While I understand the game aims for realism, in real life, you wouldn’t misplace your weapon and ammo. Backpacks typically have compartments.

The gameplay involves too much time spent organizing, loading cartridges, and cleaning, rather than focusing on the action.

Edit: A few days later, I changed the settings to custom mode to make it a bit easier, since I can’t play standing, and it takes a bit of time to reach the pouch and gun while seated. It took some pride to do it, but well, the purpose is to have fun, right? After playing a little longer, the organizing and filling cartridges didn’t bother me much. I’m actually kind of addicted to the game now. Every time I die, I reload to a little earlier. I know it’s cheating, but it made the game way more fun. Losing your backpack after you die is a big turn-off for me. I’m glad the game is so flexible, catering to both hardcore gamers and those who just want to have fun.

developer_response.body: Glad to hear that adjusting difficulty settings helped improve your overall experience, explorer! Having fun is indeed a priority. We hope you'll try ITR2 at some point in the future!

reviews.review_description: We need to make stupid weapons or cool weapons or something funny so please add it to this version ok

This game is basically stalker in vr
Would recommend
This game is one of the best vr experiences I have played, it has a story, beautiful gunplay, and a LOT of loot. Highly recommend.
Until my save file doesn't want to load no more or even the game doesn't want to load hopefully they fix it

Into the Radius is truly incredible!  The game will make you uncomfortable and nervous well out in the radius knowing anything can turn the corner to kill you but still feels fair.

The game still gives us time to breath with our base while still keeping you lonely with the thrown together warehouse.  You feel like scraps were thrown together just enough to keep you alive for the sole purpose of delivery new data to your superiors.

The guns feel great the guns jamming mid fight is terrifying but rarely annoying.  The variety of weapons is great too!  Get the weapons starting more sovient era then turning more modern is a great way to show progression as well.

This is probably the most immersive VR Game I have ever played. Got the sh*t scared out of me when I was exploring a really dark building and the door flew open with some random shadow figure. Been playing the game a lot now, probably the first game I play when I hop on. I f*cking hate the space-time anomalies, especially the wind one. Great game though, like the guns.

A beautiful beginning to the end. That's all I could really think of. I think that really sums up the game, because I know the review I wrote was about how scary it was, but it is generally beautiful. When I'm walking in the forest area, I just take a break to look at the gaping hole in the sky, cause it's kinda beautiful. The graphics also play hand in hand with the scenery to create this stunning view, but also leaving a sense of dread as you're reminded that this is a wasteland in a way. That you are alone. And that is what this game does really well, it lets you know you are alone. No NPCs at the safe area, just you and those things.

Really wish I could say the same about the second game. I also really enjoy playing that as well, but it isn't as good as the first, by a bit. But I am optimistic about the 2nd game, I think that if the Devs keep trying hard it will be great. Also really fun multiplayer. 

My favourite vr game at the moment, and definitely in top 5 of all time.

Just wanted to say how annoying it is they make you restart the entire tutorial  if you die. I understand you're probably not supposed to die in the TUTORIAL, but the extremely obscure directions when encountering the first anomoly resulted in me dying in it and being forced to do the entire boring tutorial over again. Part of this was probably on me but being able to even die in a tutorial is crazy on top of no save points. You tell me how to shoot "probes" but don't tell me what they are or what they do. Seeming to do nothing when shooting them into the anomoly i ended up walking through it and dying with no other directions. I will give the game another chance soon but i tend to have a lot less patience when it comes to vr and i don't feel like playing through an entire tutorial again because the game cant properly explain the mechanics, which is the entire point of a tutorial.

developer_response.body: Hello explorer. Tutorial has to explain a lot of different aspects of the game in a short time so yeah it definitely can feel a bit confusing or overwhelming in how much info you're given at times. Give it a few tries, do not hurry up. ITR is a quite slow and deliberate game.

reviews.review_title: One of the Best VR Experience on Quest
reviews.review_description:
The title of my post speaks for itself! The only thing missing is a more in-depth back story about what happened.

Also will we be getting the snow update along with any others?

the game is very well made with amazing ideas and atmosphere, the guns are very nice to use.
the game partially inspired an ongoing story ive been writing called Torn, so you could see how much I liked the game.
but the physics of this game single handedly make me not want to play. it is so annoying how if I drop my gun it either
1. clips through the floor
2. flies away 40 meters when it lands on the ground
very good game but oh my god the physics and the bugs are terrible

developer_response.body:
Hey explorer!

Thanks for the feedback—great to hear you've mostly enjoyed ITR1! It was made a few years back, so some physics quirks come from the engine (and how we made the game back then). We get it can be annoying, but that's just how it is. 

Save often, it might help with losing items clipping through the ground/floor.

Thanks again for the review!

reviews.review_description: Absolutely love the game already on the second

reviews.review_title: My favourite game in VR period
reviews.review_description: Stalker VR. Intense resource management and emotionally captivating experience. You feel as if you are a lonely stalker in this world. If you’re a fan of the metro books this game is a must.

I wanted to like this game but the fact I have to give myself a colonoscopy just to retrieve my handgun is annoying; not a deal-killer. The main issue I had was the fact that the mapping of the arms just didn't work. In fact the mapping of most of the body didn't work. For a shooter that's relatively fast paced I'd like to not have to hunt for my firearm.

developer_response.body:
Hey explorer!

Sorry to hear controls are giving you trouble. Try resetting your play area and ensuring good room lighting – sometimes that fixes tracking glitches (shouldn't happen often, though, giving how smart headsets now are).

reviews.review_description: The game wont start ! i click it , it shows the first logo then goes to the loft ! keeps crashing and wont start ! I JUST WANNA PLAY

developer_response.body: Hello explorer. Sorry to hear about your situation. If restarting your headset / reinstalling the game didn't help – please, visit headset settings – general – storage – find ITR in the list and clear app's data (it's a button on the left side from uninstall option.)? This should remove save files (!), but also reset the game in general to defaults.

reviews.review_description: Everything about this game to me, is super clunky. The physics don't feel comfortable or natural at all! Everything is hard to hang on to, hard to pick up, and hard to put in your belt or in your backpack! This game is so frustrating! It's impossible to reload quickly because of so many steps for everything including having to worry about if your freaking safety switch is off before shooting. WTF? You are constantly dropping stuff because it's all confusing and too complex, there needs to be a much simpler and intuitive way to interact with your equipment! I'm sorry, but this game might be fun if those issues were addressed and tuned up.

developer_response.body:
Hey explorer!

ITR isn't an arcade-style game—it has tons of items, options for placing/using them, backpack, pouches, main/secondary guns, cleaning/repair systems, different ammo types, gun attachments and more.

Most stuff just needs time to get used to. Don't rush—prep for missions slowly, figure out how guns shoot/work, bring spare mags with ammo loaded + extra boxes.

If it's still too tough, tweak in-game settings. Can't simplify core systems/items, but pick what you use/bring to the Radius. Take it easy, and it'll probably "click with you" (or it might not—not all games are for everyone!).

reviews.review_description: Will we ever get mods im about to beat the game but lately i don't really like playing i feel like it will be nice to have mods this is a great game btw

reviews.review_title: Still, my fav game on vr man.
reviews.review_description: I wish this game was still alive, and i didnt have to clear my saves llooll

This game is absolutely amazing! This game is if death stranding and resident evil had a baby. From the survival aspect of the game to the monsters and the exploration and  the overall game just feels amazing at night when I’m walking through the forest I’m always on edge when ever I’m exploring an abandoned building in the game and I hear the littlest noise I jump this game is an absolute horrifying masterpiece and would totally recommend

reviews.review_title: Cannot be played sitting down because of
reviews.review_description:
I really want to love this game, as it has a fantastic world and a breathtaking atmosphere. However, since I am a dedicated VR player who only buys games that can be played sitting down, I pay extra attention to this detail.

Unfortunately, Into the Radius cannot be played well while sitting because the belt position is not adjustable. This makes it very difficult to draw your weapon or access other items located on the belt.

I truly find it a pity that the developer is not taking action to integrate a setting into the game that allows players to customize the belt's position (height, depth, width, etc.). This would allow players to easily reach their weapon and ammunition on the belt, even when seated.

Therefore, I advise everyone against buying this game if they primarily intend to play sitting down. As long as you play while seated, you will experience more frustration than enjoyment with Into the Radius. It's a shame; the money was wasted. I don't believe the developer will implement this adjustment anymore.

developer_response.body:
Hey explorer!

Thanks for the feedback. ITR1 development ended a couple years ago, so no more updates – but I've passed your suggestions to the ITR2 team. Thanks again!

We don't agree the game is unplayable seated (VR's super personal – belt rig and turning feels fine for many), but totally get if it's not comfy for you. Appreciate you giving it a shot and sharing about your experience!

reviews.review_description: By far the best game I have played in awhile. I am not great with reviews but this is an amazing game.

Thank god the cake is year round now and not just seasonal I take the cake every were i can it is super good looking cake I never want to take a bite of it though because you don’t get infinite cake but at least I have cake thank god now I can finally talk about the game it is very good especially the weapons from a peashooter like the GSH18 to the big bulky tiger rifle all the Guns feel appropriate to the caliber it’s shooting mix that up with some very decent survival game mechanics and a ton of hidden guns items and ammo and you got yourself one of if not the best survival game on the market for VR

For quest the first one is very awesome to play!! Such an amazing job. The second title….they should have never brought to quest 3. Have to pay for the full game but only get a quarter of the game, then you have to wait a year OR TWO to get the other portions? I understand packing all this into a headset is outrageously difficult but it’s money hungry to do so. Completely erased ITR2 from pc and quest just because of how ridiculous the devs are. Take notes from the forest and red matter 2. Devs did a wonderful job and RELEASED A FULL GAME THANK YOU. ITR1 though….FANTASTIC AF!!
Quite scary, but great overall. Love the technics, gun maintenance etc.
PLEASE ADD MULTIPLAYER

I just beat this game for the first time, I had a casual 30 hour playthrough. The game looks great and I encountered no bugs or glitches. There is a bit of a learning curve if you've never played any shooters on VR, but after an hour or so you will be fumbling your equipment less. The atmosphere of the game can be spooky at times and leave you feeling tense but you will quickly learn which noises actually mean danger. Checking and cleaning your gear, packing your bag, and organizing your own space was more fun than I thought it would be, and helped me unwind after a tense run. The game is generous with its shooting range to practice all the weapons before you buy them and the weapons feature helpful upgrades. 

          Here are a few helpful tips for playing.  If you are using canned food, take it out of the can and place it in your bag to weigh less. Stick your extra ammo boxes and magazines sticking out around the "box" of the bag for easy grabbing and leaving your bag space open for loot. Repair any loot that's repairable at the bench, even loot you decide to sell as it will sell for more. The side pocket is helpful, I used it to keep spare pistols mags in a pile on the bottom and a grenade on top, empty mags go in the bag for later. I also found it helpful to leave an ammo slot on my chest empty so you can quickly stash the empty clip and grab a full one right next to it. Last but not least, smoke 'em if you got 'em. 

         Have fun explorers.

I am not a fan of scary games in vr but this is amazing I like to call it sleep deprivation the game you are the only survivor of a nuclear attack and you must save someone who you are the only one who can hear and interact with them but as you go through the radius you face void creatures and other opsticals and challenges through out the way like running out of ammo your gun jamming and needing to repair it in all its a great game would really recommend this and it’s the first time since portal 2 that I’m considering buying the second game
The best game in a long time

I have had this game for over 3 months and I have not once regretted my purchase, it’s a fun but terrifying masterpiece of a game. It does have its issues, but the level of immersion and all round fun that I’ve experienced in this game I don’t think I’ll ever replicate. When your playing for the first time don’t use tutorials just play and see what happens, learn things through trail and error not a YouTube video. Don’t listen to the 1 star reviews, if you are considering getting it buy the 2 in 1 deal and cop both games, trust me.

have to admit I love this game.The mechanics of it are spot on,Gun cleaning,things wearing down,it’s a great survival game with many aspects of horror too,plenty to do so far in 7 hours in,I love that everything you drop in game stays there,Drop a bullet you can’t find at night?go back during the day same place it’ll still be there.i love cleaning and organising my safe house.selling items is fun too upgrading your weapons etc 
All in all..great game
Tak

developer_response.body: Thanks for giving the game a try, explorer! In case of questions - do not hesitate to email us support@cm-immersive.com.

reviews.review_description: Lovely. Hour and a half in the mission then the timer goes off.been real ITR but your off my list now. Wasted my time.

developer_response.body: Hello, explorer. Not sure what timer you're referring to, but I guess you experienced an event called Tide. Tide resets the Radius, respawning you in a random spot and also respawning all entities. So maybe that's what happened?

reviews.review_title: Slow start but a masterpiece later
reviews.review_description: I got frustrated with this years ago and never played it, recently finished into the radius 2 and I wanted to try part one again since I bought it already and wow is just as good as the second part, only thing is when you start is slow and frustrating but once you level up and get more weapons and maps, you’ll love it trust me, so hang in there and don’t give up like I did back then.

This game is scary but every time I open the game I’m amazed how great it is the gun play is just fantastic. great game amazing “story” for 29:99 I could not recommend it more I have been looking paying this game for months and I’m still not bored I know into the raidius 2 is coming out soon but this is a great game to get used to the type of game play. 
This game is about (horror, guns, and, many other things) once again please get this game it’s very worth it.
